title: Semi obscure Evangelion related stuff
wikitext:
[]: Semi-obscure Evangelion related stuff             Quote Post by ReiIsTopTierWaifu » Mon Mar 12, 2018 12:01 am So I was curious about people's thoughts on the alternate live action sequence for End of Evangelion, not the one in the movie, but a cut scene which is 11:16 minutes long, and basically follows a world without Shinji and how different it would be, an alternate reality like the one shown in episode 26, yet it doesn't involve Shinji, I think it's pretty interesting seeing that Asuka has a relationship with Toji, and that it shows that she has a friendship with Rei in that universe, I think Also I thought that those EvangelionxJRA commercials were pretty cool, wheee it promotes Japanese horse racing, pretty cool animation, Schick commercials were weird, a beardless Gendo is a odd loking Gendo Also I heard of the story where Spike Spencer (voice acotr for Shinji) was asked to do an aids psa, and they needed a female, so he asked Tiffany Grant (voice actor for Asuka) they shot the psa in seperate booths, and apparently the people they sent the audio to never aired it due to it being too 'real', there's a video on YouTube called, 'Tiffany Grant interview @ Ramencon 2013' and around 7 minutes she discusses the psa, and how she has a cassetee of the audio, soooo, has anyone since then gathered any info on what happened with the audio So, what are your guys favorite weird Evangelion commercials, and sponsored aid pass?

[]: Re: Semi-obscure Evangelion related stuff             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Mar 12, 2018 8:44 am Not exactly a commercial, but I will never get over Petit Eva-style Shinji, Rei and Asuka on a Doritos package. EDIT: I found a photo of it: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Semi-obscure Evangelion related stuff             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Mar 13, 2018 7:17 pm I think the alternate live action sequence is beautiful, especially when at the end Shinji realizes how sad it is to not exist (and he has Anno's voice lol). The best Eva commercial is the one where Gendo causes an Impact by shaving (Shick x Eva).

[]: Re: Semi-obscure Evangelion related stuff             Quote Post by ReiIsTopTierWaifu » Fri Mar 23, 2018 9:10 pm Blockio wrote: Not exactly a commercial, but I will never get over Petit Eva-style Shinji, Rei and Asuka on a Doritos package. EDIT: I found a photo of it: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Semi-obscure Evangelion related stuff             Quote Post by ReiIsTopTierWaifu » Fri Mar 23, 2018 9:12 pm kuribo-04 wrote: I think the alternate live action sequence is beautiful, especially when at the end Shinji realizes how sad it is to not exist (and he has Anno's voice lol). The best Eva commercial is the one where Gendo causes an Impact by shaving (Shick x Eva).

[]: Re: Semi-obscure Evangelion related stuff             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Sat Mar 24, 2018 6:21 am ReiIsTopTierWaifu wrote: Yeah, the cut EoE sequence, right? I saw it too and it's interesting how Toji is Asuka's boyfriend as well as her not being happy with him, and I think (I think) Rei is her best friend, a world without Shinji, as well as it being a world without Gendo, Yui, Angels, Nerv, Seele, Mari...

[]: Re: Semi-obscure Evangelion related stuff             Quote Post by ReiIsTopTierWaifu » Sat Mar 24, 2018 2:57 pm silvermoonlight wrote: I always felt the alt sequence is a nod to the Butterfly Effect in that your place in the world effects people around you very deeply even if its positive or negative and that in truth no one is a lonely island in the ocean as we can all influence people in many ways even if we don't realise it so when Shinji is gone his lack of presence is like a gapping hole for all the characters in different ways. On Rei being Asuka's friend that what I thought to and that is shown in at least three of the spin off Manga's.

[]: Re: Semi-obscure Evangelion related stuff             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Sat Mar 24, 2018 3:43 pm ReiIsTopTierWaifu wrote: Is one of them girlfriend of steel 2nd, Rei and Asuka become pretty good friends by volume #6, it's cool seeing the characters befriend in eachother in the mangas instead of them having intense dislike towards a one another, and it really is both cool/sad how without Shinji the entire Evangelion world seems completely differnet
